Madagascar Periwinkle (Madagaster andohahelensis is incorrect and I will use Catharanthus roseus) is a flowering plant native to Madagascar, known for its attractive pink, white, or red flowers. The health benefits of Madagascar Periwinkle include its use in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and certain types of cancer. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, such as vinblastine and vincristine, which have been isolated for use in chemotherapy. The most common herbal preparations made with Madagascar Periwinkle include extracts and infusions, as well as dried flowers and leaves used in traditional medicine and herbal teas.
